notes:
dễ dàng = easy; not difficult. It can also mean easily when describing how something is done.

It describes tasks, situations, problems, plans — anything that doesn’t require much effort. It can stand after a noun as an adjective, or after a verb to mean “with ease.”

Strong contrast: dễ dàng (easy) ↔ khó (difficult).  
It’s stronger and more natural than just dễ when you want to emphasize smoothness or lack of struggle.

translation.en: This task is really easy to do.

exampleSentencesTranslated: Learning new words will become easy if you practice every day.
I think using a smartphone is easy.
She solved this problem in an easy way thanks to her experience.

translation.en: This task is really easy to do.

example.vi: Bài toán này rất dễ dàng.

exampleSentences: Việc học từ mới sẽ trở nên dễ dàng hơn nếu em luyện tập mỗi ngày.
Anh thấy việc sử dụng điện thoại thông minh rất dễ dàng.
Chị ấy giải quyết vấn đề này một cách dễ dàng nhờ kinh nghiệm của mình.
notes_md:
**dễ dàng** = easy; not difficult. It can also mean **easily** when describing how something is done.

It describes tasks, situations, problems, plans — anything that doesn’t require much effort. It can stand after a noun as an adjective, or after a verb to mean “with ease.”


Strong contrast: **dễ dàng** (easy) ↔ **khó** (difficult).  
It’s stronger and more natural than just **dễ** when you want to emphasize smoothness or lack of struggle.
